description: Issue #205 Cat 1+2: CID→Unicode fallback tables and encoding decoders

userStories.title: Implement Adobe-GB1, Adobe-CNS1, Adobe-Korea1 CID→Unicode tables
userStories.description: Currently only Adobe-Japan1 CID→Unicode mapping is implemented. Add built-in CID→Unicode fallback tables for the remaining three Adobe CJK systems (Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, Korean). These tables are needed when a CIDFont has no ToUnicode CMap or its ToUnicode only partially covers glyphs.
acceptanceCriteria: Adobe-GB1 (Simplified Chinese) CID→Unicode table is bundled and functional
Adobe-CNS1 (Traditional Chinese) CID→Unicode table is bundled and functional
Adobe-Korea1 (Korean) CID→Unicode table is bundled and functional
No regression on existing Adobe-Japan1 CJK PDFs
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/cid_font.rs, crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/cmap.rs. Adobe CMap resources are open source: https://github.com/niceferrari/adobe-cmap. The existing Adobe-Japan1 implementation in cid_font.rs can be used as a template. Consider embedding CID→Unicode data as static arrays or include_bytes! for efficiency. The tables map CID numbers to Unicode code points.

userStories.title: Add encoding decoders for EUC-JP, Shift-JIS, and JIS7
userStories.description: Legacy CJK PDFs use byte-sequence encodings (EUC-JP, Shift-JIS, JIS7/ISO-2022-JP) without ToUnicode CMaps. When a CIDFont references these encodings by CMap name (e.g., 90ms-RKSJ-H for Shift-JIS, EUC-H for EUC-JP), implement byte-to-Unicode decoders as fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: EUC-JP encoded CJK byte sequences are decoded to Unicode
Shift-JIS (SJIS) encoded CJK byte sequences are decoded to Unicode
JIS7 (ISO-2022-JP) variable-length byte sequences are decoded to Unicode
No regression on ToUnicode-based CIDFont PDFs
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/cid_font.rs, crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/interpreter.rs. Encoding detection: CMap name suffix indicates encoding (e.g., 90ms-RKSJ-H → Shift-JIS, EUC-H → EUC-JP). Implement decoders in pure Rust - do NOT add external encoding crates. Shift-JIS: lead byte 0x81-0x9F/0xE0-0xEF + trail byte 0x40-0x7E/0x80-0xFC. EUC-JP: lead byte 0xA1-0xFE + trail byte 0xA1-0xFE. JIS7: uses ESC sequences to switch between ASCII and JIS X 0208.

userStories.title: Fix Identity-H/V CID fallback and partial ToUnicode handling
userStories.description: Identity-H/Identity-V encoded CIDFonts sometimes have partial or malformed ToUnicode CMaps. The current parser only handles full-range Identity mappings (<0000> <FFFF>). Implement intelligent fallback: use embedded ToUnicode for mapped CIDs and direct CID→Unicode for unmapped ones (treating CID as Unicode codepoint for Identity encoding).
acceptanceCriteria: Partial ToUnicode CMaps are used for mapped CIDs with Identity fallback for unmapped CIDs
No regression on existing Identity-H PDFs
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/cmap.rs, crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/cid_font.rs. For Identity-H encoding, CID values often directly correspond to Unicode codepoints. When ToUnicode is partial, use it for mapped ranges and fall back to CID=Unicode for unmapped ranges. Also handle la-precinct-bulletin-2014-p1.pdf (Type1C/CFF font) and malformed-from-issue-932.pdf (malformed operators) - these may need CFF font parsing and better content stream error recovery respectively.
